metadata.title: Fill in the comb: how a sum becomes an integral
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL=discrete modes/bound states; CRIMSON=continuum/free states; GOLD=envelope highlight; SLATE=axes/structure
metadata.note: MORPH: comb densifying as L grows. Tie discrete modes to bound states, continuum to free states. No delta-k bookkeeping. 10-beat slate cut.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Stretch a box to infinity and the discrete spectrum melts into a continuous one.
beats.visual:
CARD: eyebrow QUANTUM MECHANICS, headline 'Stretch a box to infinity
and the discrete spectrum
melts into a continuous one.'

beats.narration_text: A particle in a box has a discrete set of allowed energies. A free particle has a continuous spectrum. Both are described by Fourier mathematics. How does the discrete sum turn into a continuous integral as the box size grows?
beats.visual:
CARD: eyebrow THE QUESTION, headline 'How does a discrete
spectrum become
a continuous one?'

beats.narration_text: A particle in a box of length L. The allowed wavenumbers are k sub n equals n times pi over L. These form a discrete comb of frequencies — equally spaced, with spacing pi over L. The Fourier series is a sum over just these teeth.
beats.visual: Horizontal k-axis. Vertical bars at k_n = n*pi/L for n=1,2,...,8 (TEAL). Label spacing = pi/L. Box diagram on left showing length L.

beats.narration_text: Now double the box length to 2L. The spacing halves: new allowed wavenumbers at n times pi over 2L. More teeth appear, filling in the gaps. The comb becomes twice as dense.
beats.visual: Same k-axis. Original bars (TEAL, lighter). New intermediate bars appear (TEAL, darker). Spacing label changes from pi/L to pi/2L.

beats.narration_text: Keep doubling. 4L, 8L, 16L. The comb teeth crowd together, the gaps shrink. The sum over discrete modes begins to look like a smooth curve. At each stage, the envelope — the smooth shape of how much each mode contributes — stays the same. Only the density of modes increases.
beats.visual: Morph animation: comb at L (sparse), 2L, 4L, 8L, becoming denser. TEAL bars growing more numerous. GOLD envelope curve stays fixed. Label shows density increasing.

beats.narration_text: In the limit L goes to infinity, the spacing delta k equals pi over L goes to zero. The discrete sum — sum over n of c sub n times e to the i k sub n x — becomes an integral: one over two pi times integral of phi of k times e to the i k x d k. This is the Fourier transform.
beats.visual: Show transition: sum symbol with bars (TEAL, left) → integral sign with continuous curve (CRIMSON, right). Arrow labeled 'L → infinity'. Formula text for both sides.

beats.narration_text: In quantum mechanics, discrete means bound. A particle in a finite box has a discrete set of energy levels, each with an allowed wavenumber. A free particle has no box, so L is effectively infinite and the energy spectrum is continuous.
beats.visual: Left panel: square well with discrete energy levels (TEAL horizontal lines). Right panel: open space with continuous shaded energy band (CRIMSON gradient). Label 'bound: discrete' and 'free: continuous'.

beats.narration_text: The Fourier series and the Fourier transform are not two different tools. They are the same construction at two extremes of the same parameter — the box size. Every quantum mechanics textbook switches from sums to integrals when it leaves the box. Now you know why.
beats.visual: Timeline: Fourier Series (TEAL, small L) ... Fourier Transform (CRIMSON, L→∞). Single horizontal line connecting them with L arrow. Label 'same mathematics, different limit'.

beats.narration_text: Concrete: a box of length L equals 1 meter. Allowed wavenumbers: 0, pi, 2pi, ... per meter — spaced 3.14 per meter. Expand to 10 meters: spacing drops to 0.31 per meter. At 1000 meters: spacing 0.003 per meter. At astronomical distances: spacing essentially zero, a true continuum.
beats.visual: Table: L=1m, delta_k=3.14/m; L=10m, delta_k=0.31/m; L=1000m, delta_k=0.003/m; L=infinity, delta_k=0 (continuum). TEAL column headers, CRIMSON last row labeled 'continuum'.

beats.narration_text: Small box: discrete spectrum, Fourier series. Big box: dense spectrum. Infinite box: continuous spectrum, Fourier transform.
beats.visual:
CARD: eyebrow QUANTUM MECHANICS, headline 'Small box: discrete.
Big box: dense.
Infinite box: continuous.'

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: Part of the Quantum Mechanics: Math for Quantum Physics series.

props.seriesTitle: Quantum Mechanics: Math for Quantum Physics
